title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: AmsPAF chronic metric — SSDs derived chronic toxicity endpoints, macroinvertebrate community integrates exposure weeks months. pipeline : add_amspaf() content-agnostic — function handles per-sample chronic chemistry. prepare_reference() function pure (cache): call reference data, pass object .
code: raw chemistry df    │    ▼  prescreen_analytes(k = 0.05)         drop never-detected analytes         auto-protects normalisation co-analytes (pH, DOC, Ca, Mg, etc.)    │    ▼  impute_chemistry(drivers = c("pH","EC","DOC"))         brms mvbind GAM; mi() for BDL/missing; cross-analyte covariance         Option B: post-hoc BDL cap; bdl_cap arg    │    ├──► prepare_reference(ref_df) → add_amspaf(reference = prep_ref)    │          per-sample AmsPAF (routine monitoring)    │    ▼  compute_chronic_chemistry(focal_dates, tau = 90, window = 365)         time-weighted geometric mean; forward-step duration weighting         expand_focal_dates("2024-01-01", "2026-01-01") for daily sequences    │    ▼  prepare_reference(chr_ref) → add_amspaf(reference = prep_chr_ref)         chronic AmsPAF (calibration target vs macroinvertebrate data)

title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
code: library(leachatetools) options(leachatetools.guideline_dir = "/path/to/guideline data")  # Stage -1: drop rarely-detected analytes included <- prescreen_analytes(chem, k = 0.05) chem_f   <- dplyr::filter(chem, analyte %in% included)  # Stage 0: impute BDL and missing values imp <- impute_chemistry(chem_f, drivers = c("pH", "EC", "DOC"))  # Per-sample AmsPAF (routine monitoring) ref_imp  <- dplyr::filter(imp, site_id == "ref") prep_ref <- prepare_reference(ref_imp) out_ps   <- add_amspaf(dplyr::filter(imp, site_id != "ref"),                        reference = prep_ref)  # Chronic AmsPAF (calibration target) bio_dates <- expand_focal_dates("2024-01-01", "2026-04-01", by = "day") chr_ds    <- compute_chronic_chemistry(dplyr::filter(imp, site_id != "ref"),                                        focal_dates = bio_dates) chr_ref   <- compute_chronic_chemistry(ref_imp, focal_dates = bio_dates) prep_chr  <- prepare_reference(chr_ref) out_chr   <- add_amspaf(chr_ds, reference = prep_chr)

previous_headings: 3. SSD fitting — what was done and why
title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: ANZECC 2000 / ANZG analytes come one two sources: Warne (2000) — original derivation data. Raw species concentrations inst/extdata/anzecc_warne2000_observations.csv. vast majority analytes (organics, metals) use source. ANZG XLSX technical briefs — modern updated datasets ANZG 2021–2026. replace ANZECC 2000 values available. XLSX files bundled package (large proprietary); must present local “guideline data/” folder fit time. Set path via: XLSX analytes: NH3-N, NO3-N (3 classes), B, Cr, Cu, Ni, Zn. Ni special case: nickel XLSX (col 31) holds raw test concentrations, MLR-normalised values ANZG used derive DGVs. 26 normalised negligible-effect values Stauber et al. (2021) Table 3 (index conditions: pH 7.5, Ca 6 mg/L, Mg 4 mg/L, DOC 0.5 mg/L) pre-tabulated inst/extdata/ni_mlr_normalised_table3.csv.
code: options(leachatetools.guideline_dir = "/path/to/guideline data")

previous_headings: 3. SSD fitting — what was done and why
title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: method = “multi” (default ssd_paf()): Fits 6 BCANZ-recommended distributions model-averages result: lnorm, llogis, burrIII3, lgumbel, gamma, weibull. current best practice produces good results validated analytes regardless original derivation method. method = “anzecc”: Uses per-analyte distribution recorded fit_dist column inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. attempts replicate original ANZG derivation closely possible. Useful back-checking published DGVs. Key distribution aliases metadata: - burrIII3 — Burr Type III; Burrlioz 2.0 default; used ANZECC 2000 analytes modern ANZG briefs. - lgumbel — log-Gumbel = Fréchet = inverse Weibull (distributional family, different names different software). Burrlioz calls “inverse Weibull”; ssdtools calls “lgumbel”. Used NO3-N soft hard water. - multi — expand full 6-distribution set (method=“multi”). Used NH3-N Cr ANZG used shinyssdtools BCANZ dists.

previous_headings: 3. SSD fitting — what was done and why
title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: Nitrate toxicity physically modulated water hardness (calcium/magnesium ions compete nitrate ion-exchange sites). species can 150× less sensitive NO3-N hard water vs soft water — mechanistic effect, species-assemblage difference. hardness changes actual effect individual organism, valid biological interpolation hardness classes; ANZG derived three completely separate SSDs. three analyte names used throughout package: Callers can either supply explicit class name pass analyte = "NO3-N" hardness_mg_L let ssd_paf() / ssd_lookup() resolve class. Known problem — class-boundary discontinuity: measured hardness near boundary (e.g. 28 vs 31 mg/L), measurement noise causes abrupt jumps modelled PAF change actual conditions. probabilistic smoothing approach designed yet implemented (see §5.1).

title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: Analytes ANZECC derived triggers acute LC50/EC50 data using Maximum Residue (MR) method trigger_divisor (= ACR) > 1. fitting code divides concentrations ACR fitting, resulting SSD chronic-equivalent scale. ssd_paf(conc_ug_L = ...) therefore accepts measured chronic-equivalent concentration directly — ACR adjustment needed caller.

title: leachatetools — Handover Notes
text: Validated dashboard/R/make_ssd_validation.R published ANZG/ANZECC DGVs. ratio calc/ref close 1.0; deviations reflect ssdtools vs Burrlioz 2.0 algorithm differences. Summary (ssdtools 2.6, method = “anzecc” / per-analyte dist): Cr 15% gap (HC5 ratio = 0.856): data extraction confirmed correct (13 species, matching ANZG Table .1 exactly). gap arises ANZG used shinyssdtools v0.4.0 use ssdtools 2.6; model-averaging weights differ versions. Accepted version artefact; SSD otherwise sound. Parked analytes: Chlorpyrifos Diazinon large HC5 discrepancies explained simple version differences. excluded SSD pipeline treated “model available”. See data-raw/analyte_metadata_parked.csv data TODO notes.

text: Design complete; implementation TODO stub paf.R .no3_class(). idea: treat measured hardness log-normally distributed measurement CV (default 5%) compute probability belonging hardness class. Weight three PAF values class probability. requires fitting / loading three NO3-N models every call near boundary, cache warm-started three session init NO3-N analyte list.
code: sdlog  <- sqrt(log(1 + cv^2)) p_soft <- plnorm(30,  log(h), sdlog) p_hard <- 1 - plnorm(150, log(h), sdlog) p_mod  <- 1 - p_soft - p_hard # For ssd_paf(): expected_paf <- p_soft * paf_soft + p_mod * paf_mod + p_hard * paf_hard

title: leachatetools — project TODO
text: Goal: carry uncertainty end--end — imputation, normalisation, SSD lookup, CA/RA mixture combination, temporal aggregation — expose package boundary downstream consumers (beyond package) can keep propagating . Current state: uncertainty exists pieces collapsed early. - impute_chemistry(return = "draws") already emits posterior draws, chronic/AmsPAF pipeline consumes point estimates (return = "point"). - ssd_paf() can return CIs (ci = TRUE) AmsPAF path uses point PAFs. - analytic CA combination (compute_ca_group_mspaf) uses single sigma per group uncertainty HC50/sigma . Design questions settle implementing: - Representation: posterior draws (Monte Carlo, composable, heavy) vs. parametric moments (mean + variance, light, needs propagation rule step). Draws honest default given imputation already Bayesian. - Plumbing: thread draw_id dimension time_weighted_aggregate() add_amspaf() single posterior draw flows coherently chemistry → PAF → msPAF → time-average, summarise end. - SSD parameter uncertainty: draw HC50/HC05 (hence sigma) fitted ssdtools model rather treating fixed. - Boundary output: return per-endpoint posterior draws (quantiles) external risk model can ingest . substantial refactor — deliberately, likely imputation generalisation (§6) lands draws interface stable.

title: leachatetools — project TODO
text: CA mixture-slope (sigma) formula — RESOLVED & FIXED (2026-06). Confirmed primary source (De Zwart & Posthuma 2005, eq. 6, references/Zwart.Posthuma2005.pdf p.2672). canonical CA combination msPAF_CA = Φ(log10(ΣTU) / σ̄) σ̄ = mean(σ) — plain arithmetic average component SSD slopes (β̄_TMoA; β = √3·σ/π), TU-weighted mean old sqrt(sum(w²σ²)) variance form. HUs/TUs summed (non-log) combined PAF read one log-normal CDF. compute_ca_group_mspaf() now uses sigma_mix <- mean(sigma); pinned tests/testthat/test-mspaf-ca.R. TMoA / mode--action grouping — RESOLVED (hybrid, 2026-06-02). bundled moa_group column now populated substance’s stated mechanism action (per ANZG/Warne guidance doc), mapped onto De Zwart & Posthuma (2005, Table 3/4) TMoA vocabulary: respiratory + ROS, gill-dependent), Zn (Ca2+ uptake disruption gill) → ionoregulatory group; explicitly state Cr(III) “evidence specific mode action” B acts passive membrane diffusion species-specific effects → correctly solo. Ni least-pure member (respiratory/ROS alongside ionoregulatory) ionoregulatory primary. ammonia nitrate group. Ammonia/nitrate checked NEW ANZG tech briefs (PDF, Warne HTML): ammonia §2.1 = gill structural damage + uncoupling oxidative phosphorylation + CNS ATP depletion + osmoregulatory disruption (metal-style gill ion-uptake competition); nitrate §2.1 = methaemoglobinaemia (O2-pigment conversion) salinity-driven osmoregulatory effects, gills low-permeability NO3. confirmed distinct ionoregulatory-metal mechanism → solo. Organics CA-grouped shared mechanism: nonpolar narcosis (PAHs, BTEX, HCB), polar narcosis (phenol, 2-chlorophenol), uncoupler oxidative phosphorylation (2,4,6-TCP, pentachlorophenol), AChE inhibition: organophosphate (parathion, azinphos-methyl, dimethoate, demeton-S-methyl), neurotoxicant: cyclodiene (aldrin, dieldrin, endrin, g-BHC/lindane), neurotoxicant: DDT (DDT, DDE, methoxychlor). Users can override whole scheme supplying metadata CSV. Doc comment R/mspaf.R updated; classification lives inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. Hg+Se CA-combined shared sulfhydryl-binding TMoA (docs cite sulfhydryl/enzyme disruption). Possible future refinement: Cr/B/Mn currently stay solo ANZG docs state clear primary mode — revisit better mechanism data emerge (e.g. whether Cr(III) belongs ionoregulatory group). speciation default — RESOLVED (2026-06-02). .SSD_NAME_MAP keeps unspeciated "" → "As_V", rationale now corrected verified: conservative max-PAF envelope two speciation SSDs, redox/oxygenation assumption. Empirically (checked 0.1–5000 µg/L) (V) SSD yields higher PAF (III) SSD every realistic concentration — cross ~10 mg/L dissolved . even though landfill leachate reducing ((III) dominant), (III) oxidises slowly freshwater, (III) toxic single -sensitive diatom, whole-assemblage (V) SSD sits lower (lower tail pulled corrected algal acute points) → (V) conservative regardless speciation. Comment R/paf.R + CSV note updated; pinned tests/testthat/test-paf--envelope.R (fails loudly future SSD-data revision ever lets (III) overtake (V), point switch true runtime max--envelope). Users can still pass (III)/(V) explicitly.

title: leachatetools — project TODO
text: Probabilistic NO3-N hardness weighting — RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (R/paf.R). ssd_paf("NO3-N", hardness_mg_L=) now blends soft/moderate/hard SSDs hardness-class probability instead snapping one class: p_soft = plnorm(30, log(h), s), p_hard = 1 - plnorm(150, log(h), s), p_mod = 1 - p_soft - p_hard, s = sqrt(log(1+cv^2)), default cv = 0.05 (.no3_weights()); ExpectedPAF = Σ w·PAF_class. Continuous across class boundaries (h=150 PAF mean mod/hard PAFs); pinned test-paf-values.R. ssd_hc50() / CA mixture step still use single representative class (.no3_class()); revisit CA needs blend . Benchmark three BDL/imputation configs — IMPLEMENTED + first benchmark 2026-06-04. three configs now fit_imputation_model(impute_method=) option ("rescor_mi" default, "cens", "cens_factor"); see §10b . rescor = TRUE + mi() + post-hoc DL cap (current = "rescor_mi"). rescor = FALSE + cens("left") (clean BDL; coupling = "cens"). rescor = FALSE + cens("left") + shared (1 | sample_id) factor (= "cens_factor"). First hold-benchmark B.S01 routine metals (/Cr/Cu/Ni/Pb/Zn, 89 samples, 19 masked detected cells censored 2x truth, log10 scale): | method | RMSE | MAE | 90% coverage | |——–|——|—–|————–| | rescor_mi | 0.18 | 0.15 | n/(draws OOM) | | cens | 0.54 | 0.38 | 0.89 | | cens_factor | 0.49 | 0.36 | 0.89 | Ordering expected — recovery improves cross-analyte coupling strength (full rescor > shared factor > none) — benchmark supports keeping rescor_mi default. Caveats: n = 19 (single random mask, one seed -> noisy); DL cap can give rescor_mi small edge (never raises error); rescor_mi’s posterior draws hit 16 GB memory limit (coverage, draws costly §1 uncertainty work), whereas cens methods give well-calibrated intervals cheaply. Next: repeated CV / multiple seeds, disable cap fully fair point comparison. (Benchmark scripts live gitignored test data/.) Robustness gap found benchmarking: dataset ~100 mostly -BDL organics (several near-undetected metals), fit_imputation_model builds huge organics/metals model (107-way -> Stan parser failure; 18-way metals -> intractable). Add min-detection-frequency filter metals / organics target groups (drop analytes detect-count/frequency floor), analogous prescreen_analytes() imputation targets. BDL cap inspection (.check_bdl_imputed, R/impute.R). cap clips imputed BDL cells detection limit; sites whose chemistry legitimately implies high concentrations cap can fire often. Surface summary cap activations regime auditable.

title: Detecting leachate with the Leachate Mixing Fraction (LMF)
text: add_lmf() asks single question water sample: fraction sample’s chemistry can explained mixture local reference water landfill leachate? answer — leachate mixing fraction (LMF) — reported percentage: 0 means sample chemically indistinguishable local reference water, 100 means matches leachate end-member. source-detection index, deliberately separate toxicity: sample can carry clear leachate signature (high LMF) still well guideline values, vice versa. LMF built classic two-component end-member mixing model used catchment hydrochemistry (Christophersen & Hooper, 1992). title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in leachatetools
text: ANZG freshwater toxicant guideline values derived specific index water-chemistry condition. ambient water chemistry differs condition, guideline — therefore effective toxicity — changes. leachatetools adjusts measured concentrations index-condition equivalents computing PAF values samples compared basis. adjustment : Cnorm=Cfield×EC10indexEC10fieldC_\text{norm} = C_\text{field} \times \frac{\text{EC10}_\text{index}}{\text{EC10}_\text{field}} normalised concentration enters SSD lookup. value Cnorm>CfieldC_\text{norm} > C_\text{field} means measured concentration toxic site conditions concentration index conditions; Cnorm<CfieldC_\text{norm} < C_\text{field} means less toxic. Normalisation formulas stored bundled analyte metadata CSV (inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv). can inspect override supplying CSV via analyte_metadata argument prepare_reference() add_amspaf().

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in leachatetools
text: Source: ANZG (2021) ammonia freshwater DGV technical brief. Index condition: pH 7.0, temperature 20 °C. Required co-analytes: pH, temperature (°C). NH₃-N toxicity driven un-ionised fraction, increases sharply pH temperature. normalisation converts field total ammonia-N equivalent concentration index condition using ionisation equilibrium: fun(pH,T)=11+10pKa(T)−pHf_\text{un}(\text{pH}, T) = \frac{1}{1 + 10^{\,pK_a(T) - \text{pH}}} pKa(T)=0.09018+2729.92/(T+273.15)pK_a(T) = 0.09018 + 2729.92 / (T + 273.15). Water temperature must supplied analyte = "temperature" rows chemistry data frame. Use estimate_water_temp() air temperature records available.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in leachatetools
text: Source: ANZG (2024) copper freshwater DGV draft technical brief, Figure 3. Index condition: DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analyte: DOC (mg/L). Cnorm=C×(0.5DOC)0.977C_\text{norm} = C \times \left(\frac{0.5}{\text{DOC}}\right)^{0.977} DOC clamped [0.5, 30] mg/L applying formula: Upper cap (30 mg/L): formula validated 30 mg/L DOC; use DGV 30 mg/L cases consider site-specific assessment. Lower cap (0.5 mg/L): index condition DOC, formula extrapolates unvalidated range; cap prevents unbounded upward correction.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in leachatetools
text: Source: ANZG (2024) nickel freshwater DGV draft technical brief; invertebrate-trophic-level MLR Peters et al. (2021). Index condition: pH 7.5, Ca 6 mg/L, Mg 4 mg/L, DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analytes: pH, DOC, Ca, Mg (mg/L Ca Mg). Valid range: pH 6.0–8.5, Ca 2–70 mg/L, Mg 1.6–54 mg/L, DOC 0.5–20 mg/L. Peters et al. (2021) MLR invertebrates: log⁡e(EC10)=S+2.09log⁡e[DOC]+0.19log⁡e[Ca]+0.40log⁡e[Mg]−0.40pH−0.24log⁡e[DOC]⋅pH\log_e(\text{EC10}) = S + 2.09\log_e[\text{DOC}] + 0.19\log_e[\text{Ca}] + 0.40\log_e[\text{Mg}] - 0.40\,\text{pH} - 0.24\log_e[\text{DOC}]\cdot\text{pH} sensitivity coefficient SS cancels field--index ratio, : Cnorm=C×exp⁡(MLR(index)−MLR(field))C_\text{norm} = C \times \exp\!\Big(\text{MLR}(\text{index}) - \text{MLR}(\text{field})\Big) Important limitation: ANZG Ni DGVs derived using trophic-level-specific MLRs algae, aquatic plants, invertebrates fish applied species SSD. invertebrate MLR used appropriate single model macroinvertebrate-focused assessment, exactly reproduce published lookup-table DGVs, based full multi-trophic SSD re-derivation water chemistry combination. Users whose assessments span full range trophic levels may wish supply geometric-mean correction across four MLRs via custom analyte metadata CSV.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in leachatetools
text: Source: ANZG (2024) zinc freshwater DGV draft technical brief; Daphnia magna (Arthropoda / crustacean) MLR Gadd et al. (prep.). Index condition: pH 7.5, hardness 30 mg/L CaCO₃, DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analytes: pH, hardness (mg/L CaCO₃), DOC (mg/L). Valid range: pH 6.2–8.3, hardness 20–440 mg/L CaCO₃, DOC 0.5–15 mg/L. D. magna MLR: log⁡e(EC10)=S−0.52pH+0.31log⁡e[hardness]−1.4log⁡e[DOC]+0.24log⁡e[DOC]⋅pH\log_e(\text{EC10}) = S - 0.52\,\text{pH} + 0.31\log_e[\text{hardness}] - 1.4\log_e[\text{DOC}] + 0.24\log_e[\text{DOC}]\cdot\text{pH} Note lower pH → higher Zn DGV (Zn less bioavailable low pH H⁺ competes Zn²⁺ biotic ligands). pH DOC clamped published valid range applying formula. Limitation — accuracy degrades pH 8: published Zn DGVs derived applying four trophic-level MLRs 31 species re-fitting SSD water chemistry combination. D. magna model alone gives reasonable approximation low neutral pH (within ~15% lookup-table DGV), diverges substantially high pH. pH 8.3, Daphnia-model-derived DGV approximately 2.4 µg/L versus published 1.0 µg/L, highly sensitive Chlorella sp. (PNG isolate, EC10 = 0.91 µg/L index) Raphidocelis subcapitata stronger negative pH coefficients dominate lower tail SSD alkaline pH. practical consequence: high-pH receiving waters (> 8.0), formula underestimate Zn toxicity treated caution. Users assessing alkaline sites consider supplying geometric-mean correction across four trophic-level MLRs via custom analyte metadata CSV.

title: leachatetools
text: leachatetools analyses water quality freshwater systems potentially impacted landfill leachate. turns routine monitoring chemistry ecologically interpretable, mixture-aware risk estimates, explicit handling -detection data local background conditions. package three pillars: Leachate detection (LMF). End-member mixing analysis (add_lmf()) estimates leachate-mixing fraction sample major-ion signature, distinguishing leachate influence natural variation. Chemistry imputation. Bayesian multivariate model (fit_imputation_model() / impute_chemistry()) imputes -detection missing analyte concentrations preserving cross-analyte correlation structure, can return full posterior draws. Multi-substance toxicity (AmsPAF). Species sensitivity distributions (ssd_paf()) feed multi-substance potentially-affected-fraction (add_amspaf()) combines toxicants concentration- response-addition subtracts local background via Added Risk Approach. Toxicity grounded ANZECC/ANZG freshwater guideline datasets, bioavailability physicochemical normalisations (hardness, pH, DOC, temperature) applied every sample compared index-condition basis.

title: Compute the Adjusted multi-substance PAF (AmsPAF) for water quality samples — add_amspaf
text: df Long-format monitoring dataframe. Required columns: sample_id, site_id, analyte, value (concentrations µg/L). Optional recommended: datetime (propagated AmsPAF rows present), detected (assumed TRUE absent), imputed (logical; present, n_analytes_imputed populated output). Driver analytes needed chemistry normalisation (e.g. pH, DOC) present rows df. reference Background reference chemistry ARA adjustment. Accepts three forms: prepared_reference object prepare_reference() — normalisation already applied; used directly. raw long-format data frame (schema df) — passed prepare_reference() internally. NULL (default) — ARA adjustment; raw concentrations assessed directly SSDs. analyte_metadata Data frame analyte metadata, NULL load bundled inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. Passed prepare_reference() derive_ssd_params(). method SSD method passed ssd_hc50() ssd_paf(). "multi" (default) fits 6 BCANZ distributions model-averages; "anzecc" uses per-analyte distribution matching original ANZG derivation. guideline_dir Path "guideline data" folder containing ANZG XLSX files. Falls back getOption("leachatetools.guideline_dir"). min_analytes Minimum number analytes fitted SSDs required compute AmsPAF sample. Default 3. ref_summary Summary statistic reference distribution reference raw data frame.  Passed prepare_reference().  Default "geom_mean" — maximum-likelihood central tendency log-normal concentrations, PICT-consistent estimate "typical" exposure resident community adapted .  options: "median", "arith_mean", "p80", "p90", "p95". require_temperature Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, sample reports NH3-N measurement must also carry water temperature row (ammonia un-ionised-fraction normalisation undefined without ); missing temperature hard error rather silent drop ammonia. Supply temperature via direct measurement, derive estimate_water_temp() (optionally fed get_silo_air_temp()). Set FALSE datasets assess ammonia.

title: Compute the Leachate Mixing Fraction (LMF) for water quality samples — add_lmf
text: df Long-format dataframe. Required columns: sample_id, site_id, analyte, value, detected, datetime. leachate_data Optional long-format dataframe structure df providing leachate end-member chemistry. Required columns: sample_id, analyte, value, detected. supplied, samples used build leachate end-member regardless date regardless total-N content (total-N quality filter bypassed — supply curated data). Samples missing required LMF panel analytes silently dropped. NULL (default) uses standard leachate feature detection logic (requires dashboard infrastructure: feature_df(), data_df()). reference_data Optional long-format dataframe structure df providing reference site chemistry end-member calibration. Required columns: sample_id, analyte, value, detected. supplied, samples used build reference end-member regardless date; calibration window ignored. end-member applied globally features. NULL (default) uses standard per-feature matched reference site logic (requires dashboard infrastructure: feature_sfc(), data_df()). lmf_analyte_uuid UUID assigned LMF rows uuid.analyte column output. Set fixed UUID integrating data system tracks analytes UUID. Default NA_character_. calibration_window_years Number years calibration data use. Window centred input data's date range shifted backwards future data unavailable. Default 5. min_leachate_total_n_mgl Minimum total N (mg/L, sum NH4 + NO3 + NO2) leachate sample qualify end-member calibration. Excludes non-representative samples (dilution events, treatment upsets). Total N used rather NH4-N alone aerated leachate features may nitrified NH4 NO3. Default 20. rsd_default Default relative analytical uncertainty, applied sigma_meas = rsd_default * |x| floor rsd_default * |R|. Default 0.05 (5% RSD). min_ref_samples Minimum reference samples required calibration window. Features matched reference site fewer samples skipped silently. Default 10. min_leachate_samples Minimum valid leachate samples required end-member construction. function stops informative error met. Default 10. max_sigma_lsi Maximum permitted sigma_lmf percentage points (uncertainty mixing fraction estimate). Samples threshold return NA reason code "insufficient_precision". Default 10 (.e., ±10 percentage points). max_chi2_per_df Maximum permitted chi-squared per degree freedom (computed original inverse-variance weights, robust reweighting). Retained diagnostic output cases. Default Inf (hard gate — robust reweighting handles outlier ions directly, making hard chi2 gate redundant samples). informativeness_threshold Threshold sigma_R / |L - R| classifying ions high-information. Computed calibration data, per-sample. Lower values stringent. Default 0.20. min_high_info_ions Minimum number high-information ions must measured sample LMF computed. Samples threshold return NA reason code "insufficient_high_info_ions". Default 3L. robust_iterations Number Huber M-estimator reweighting passes. pass, ions whose residuals exceed robust_threshold_k *   MAD(residuals) downweighted proportionally. Three iterations sufficient convergence practice. Set 0L disable robust reweighting entirely. Default 3L. robust_threshold_k Threshold multiplier applied unweighted median absolute deviation (MAD) per-ion residuals. Ions |r_i| > k * MAD estimation weight reduced factor k * MAD / |r_i|. Smaller values downweight aggressively; k = 1.5 fairly tight, k = 2.5 close conventional Huber constant. Default 1.5. verbose TRUE, prints ion informativeness table via cli::cli_inform() showing ion's score high/low classification. Useful tuning informativeness_threshold. Default FALSE.

title: Estimate water temperature from air temperature measurements — estimate_water_temp
text: Water temperature lags air temperature seasonally (thermal hysteresis): air temperature, water tends cooler spring warmer autumn. -day air-regression averages loop. Adding first-harmonic day--year term — sin(2*pi*doy/365.25) cos(2*pi*doy/365.25) — lets regression represent . Day--year cyclic, must enter harmonics, raw linear term. seasonal = "auto" candidate models fitted compared AICc (Akaike Information Criterion small-sample correction); lower-AICc model used. AICc — -sample R² RMSE — selection rule, larger model's -sample fit can improve always chosen even overfits. seasonal model eligible least seasonal_min_n observations spanning least seasonal_min_quarters quarters; otherwise air-model used.

title: Estimate water temperature from air temperature measurements — estimate_water_temp
code: set.seed(1) air <- tibble::tibble(   datetime        = seq(as.Date("2020-01-01"), as.Date("2022-12-31"), by = "day") ) air$air_temp_mean_C <- rnorm(nrow(air), mean = 15, sd = 8) wt_obs <- tibble::tibble(   datetime     = sample(air$datetime, 80),   water_temp_C = NA_real_ ) wt_obs$water_temp_C <-   air$air_temp_mean_C[match(wt_obs$datetime, air$datetime)] * 0.85 + 2 +   rnorm(80, 0, 1) wt <- estimate_water_temp(air, wt_obs) #> ℹ Selected air-water model: air + season (day-of-year harmonic). R² = 0.976, #>   RMSE = 1.01 °C, AICc = 239.4 (n = 80). #>   AICc comparison (lower preferred): air only = 243.7, air + season = 239.4. attr(wt, "model")  # which model was selected (air-only vs air + season) #> [1] "air + season (day-of-year harmonic)"

what: leachatetools: Water Quality Analysis Tools for Leachate-Impacted Waters — leachatetools-package
title: leachatetools: Water Quality Analysis Tools for Leachate-Impacted Waters — leachatetools-package
text: Tools detecting assessing landfill-leachate impacts freshwater quality, organised around three capabilities. First, end-member mixing analysis estimates leachate-mixing fraction ('LMF') sample major-ion signature, distinguishing leachate influence natural variation. Second, Bayesian multivariate model imputes -detection missing analyte concentrations preserving cross-analyte correlation structure. Third, species-sensitivity- distribution ('SSD') based potentially-affected-fraction ('PAF') toxicity assessment, including multi-substance PAF ('msPAF' / 'AmsPAF') combines toxicants concentration response addition subtracts local background via Added Risk Approach. Toxicity derived ANZECC/ANZG freshwater guideline datasets, bioavailability physicochemical normalisations (hardness, pH, dissolved organic carbon temperature) applied samples compared common index-condition basis.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: df Long-format chemistry data frame.  Required columns: sample_id (character), analyte (character), detected (logical). group_by_feature = TRUE, also requires site_id (character). k Minimum detection frequency (proportion, 0–1). Analytes n_detected / n_samples < k excluded unless protected. Default 0.05 (5 %). protect Optional character vector additional analyte names protect prescreen exclusion, top metadata-derived co-analytes.  Default NULL.  Pass required_vars downstream imputation step . potency_keep Logical. Enable potency escape hatch (keep frequency-failing analyte whose concentration reaches guideline value). Default TRUE. Set FALSE frequency-prescreen. potency_frac Numeric (>= 0). Fraction 95 % guideline value (dgv_95pct_ug_L) detected concentration must reach rescue analyte. Default 1 (must reach guideline). Lower (e.g. 0.5) precautionary. group_by_feature Logical. TRUE, detection frequency computed per site_id analyte included passes features (worst-case feature, pooled counts). FALSE (default), frequency pooled across samples. analyte_metadata Data frame analyte metadata, NULL load bundled inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. Used identify co-analytes must protected exclusion. return Either "vector" (default) return character vector included analyte names, "table" return tibble one row per analyte showing detection statistics inclusion flag.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: return = "vector": character vector passing analyte names. vector carries attribute "excluded" listing analytes pass threshold (non-protected ), callers can record dropped. return = "table": tibble columns analyte, n_samples, n_detected, detect_freq, limiting_site, protected (logical), potency_kept (logical; rescued potency escape hatch), included (logical).  group_by_feature = TRUE, n_samples / n_detected / detect_freq describe limiting site (worst-case feature drove inclusion decision) limiting_site names ; group_by_feature = FALSE, counts pooled across samples limiting_site NA.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: Analytes listed coanalytes_required bundled metadata (e.g. pH, DOC, Ca, Mg, hardness, temperature) automatically protected exclusion regardless detection frequency, needed chemistry normalisation add_amspaf().  Additional analytes can protected via protect argument (typical use: pass required_vars intend use fit_imputation_model() vars survive prescreen).  Protected analytes fall threshold reported separately caller aware. Potency escape hatch. Frequency alone can screen rare--potent toxicant (e.g. pesticide detected 2 % samples ecotoxicologically significant concentrations). potency_keep = TRUE (default), analyte fails frequency threshold still kept detected concentration reaches potency_frac times 95 %-species- protection guideline value (dgv_95pct_ug_L metadata). needs numeric value column units guideline (µg/L); analytes carry guideline value (toxicants) can rescued way, major ions analytes guideline unaffected.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
code: included <- prescreen_analytes(leachate_demo(), k = 0.05) included                    # analytes retained #>  [1] "CO3-CaCO3"   "Ca"          "Cl"          "Cu"          "DOC"         #>  [6] "F"           "HCO3-CaCO3"  "K"           "Mg"          "NH3-N"       #> [11] "NO2-N"       "NO3-N"       "Na"          "SO4²⁻"       "Zn"          #> [16] "hardness"    "pH"          "temperature" #> attr(,"excluded") #> character(0) attr(included, "excluded")  # see what was dropped (and why) #> character(0)

title: Estimate the fraction of species potentially affected at a concentration. — ssd_paf
text: analyte Character. Analyte name (key .SSD_NAME_MAP). Supply "NO3-N" together hardness_mg_L automatic class selection, supply explicit class name ("NO3-N_soft" etc.). conc_ug_L Numeric. Concentration µg/L (external physicochemical corrections). method Character. "multi" (default) fits 6 BCANZ distributions model-averages; "anzecc" uses per-analyte distribution best matches original ANZG derivation. hardness_mg_L Numeric NULL. Required NO3-N analyte. Hardness mg/L CaCO3 time measurement. hardness_cv Numeric. CV hardness measurement, used weight three NO3-N hardness-class SSDs probabilistically (log-normal blend smooths soft/moderate/hard boundaries). Default 0.05 (5%). Set 0 recover hard class cutoffs. guideline_dir Character. Path "guideline data" folder containing ANZG XLSX files. Falls back getOption("leachatetools.guideline_dir"). nboot Integer. Bootstrap replicates CI. 0 = CI. level Numeric. Confidence level (default 0.95).

code: time_weighted_aggregate(   df,   focal_dates,   tau_days = 90,   window_days = 365,   summary = c("geom_mean", "arith_mean", "p90"),   anchor_outside_window = TRUE,   eps = 1e-09 )

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
text: df Long-format data frame.  Required columns: sample_id (character) — unique sample identifier site_id (character) — monitoring feature identifier datetime (Date POSIXct) — sample collection date/time analyte (character) — analyte / index name value (numeric) — concentration index value detected (logical) — whether value detected observation Optional: imputed (logical) — propagated n_imputed_in_window. focal_dates Either Date vector (applied features df) data frame columns focal_date (Date) site_id (character) specifying per-feature focal dates. tau_days Exponential-decay rate parameter days.  Default 90. effective half-life tau_days * log(2) ≈ 62 days default. Choose match response timescale downstream biology (algae: days–weeks; macroinvertebrates: weeks–months; fish: months–years). window_days Look-back window days.  Default 365. summary Aggregation method: "geom_mean" (default), "arith_mean", "p90".  See Choosing summary . anchor_outside_window Logical (default TRUE).  TRUE, recent sample window start included anchor provide duration weighting leading edge window. duration clipped start window_start contributes -window portion interval. eps Small positive guard added inside log geometric mean avoid log(0). Default 1e-9.

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
text: long-format tibble columns: focal_date (Date) site_id (character) sample_id (character) — synthetic key "chronic_<focal_date>_<site>" analyte (character) value (numeric) — chronic time-weighted value detected (logical) — always TRUE n_samples_in_window (integer) n_imputed_in_window (integer) — count imputed == TRUE samples contributing; 0 imputed column absent df

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
text: function value-agnostic: pass long-format data frame analyte value columns compute one time-weighted value per (focal_date × site_id × analyte).  Use aggregate raw chemistry (analyte = chemical species, summary = "geom_mean") per-sample AmsPAF values (analyte = "AmsPAF", summary = "arith_mean") chronic predictor. Forward-step duration weighting treats sample representing period collection date next sample's date (focal_date recent sample).  corrects pulse-biased sampling storm events sampled frequently base-flow periods, otherwise -weight episodic concentrations. Exponential-decay temporal weighting assigns higher weight recent samples, half-life approximately tau_days * log(2) days. Combined weight sample w_i = Δt_i × exp(-(focal_date - midpoint_i) / tau_days), midpoint_i midpoint sample 's representative interval. Choosing summary: "geom_mean": max-likelihood central tendency log-normal data. Use chemistry concentrations.  Caveat: highly pulsed exposure non-linear SSD response, underestimates time-averaged risk metric (Jensen's inequality upper tail SSD). "arith_mean": weighted arithmetic mean.  Use bounded indices like AmsPAF percentages, chemistry comparison arithmetic-mean compliance trigger wanted. "p90": 90th percentile (duration-weighted empirical CDF).  Diagnostic "upper-end exposure community sees time." chronic AmsPAF specifically, recommended pipeline add_amspaf() per-sample chemistry, time_weighted_aggregate() resulting AmsPAF rows summary = "arith_mean".  computes time-averaged AmsPAF, integrates toxic response time (consistent biological communities respond fluctuating exposure) — rather AmsPAF computed single time-averaged chemistry, can substantially -state risk pulsed exposures.

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
code: # Chronic (time-weighted geometric-mean) chemistry for one downstream # analyte at two focal dates, from the bundled demo data. cu <- subset(leachate_demo(), site_id == "downstream" & analyte == "Cu") time_weighted_aggregate(   cu,   focal_dates = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-12-01")),   tau_days = 90, window_days = 365, summary = "geom_mean" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   focal_date site_id    sample_id     analyte value detected n_samples_in_window #>   <date>     <chr>      <chr>         <chr>   <dbl> <lgl>                  <int> #> 1 2024-06-01 downstream chronic_2024… Cu       7.56 TRUE                       3 #> 2 2024-12-01 downstream chronic_2024… Cu       7.50 TRUE                       6 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: n_imputed_in_window <int>

title: Convert analyte concentrations to milliequivalents per litre — to_meq
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   analyte             = "Ca",   value               = 40.08,   # mg/L   units.analyte       = "mg/L",   valence.analyte     = 2,   atomic_mass.analyte = 40.08    # g/mol ) to_meq(df)   # appends a "Ca_" row of 2 meq/L (1 mmol/L x |valence| = 2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   analyte value units.analyte valence.analyte atomic_mass.analyte #>   <chr>   <dbl> <chr>                   <dbl>               <dbl> #> 1 Ca       40.1 mg/L                        2                40.1 #> 2 Ca_       2   mg/L                        2                40.1
